text:
Team GBwill be desperate to add to their growing tally of gold medals as theOlympicsenters its seventh day. There were no British golds on Thursday but the women's four secured silver and there were bronze medals in both the men's four and the women's double sculls.

Another action-packed schedule on Friday will present gold medal opportunities in swimming, rowing and diving. Emily Craig and Imogen Grant are the big favourites in the women’s lightweight double sculls after breezing through their heats, with the final set to take place before midday.

The athletics events will also get underway with Keely Hodgkinson among the main contenders in the women's 800 metres. Elsewhere at the Games, swimming sensation Leon Marchand is looking to complete a perfect home Olympics as he targets a fourth gold in the pool.

Follow Express Sport's live updates below...

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

That's another dramatic day at the Olympics for Team GB, and one that brought three more gold medals.

We'll be back from early tomorrow with all the latest news and results.

France set up an Olympic men's football semi-final with Egypt by beating Argentina 1-0 ibut the game was marred by ugly scenes at the final whistle.

Players and coaches from both sides clashed as animosity spilled over. Crystal Palace striker Jean-Philippe Mateta got France's winner when he headed home Michael Olise's fifth-minute corner.



The Czech Republic have become the first nation to secure a gold medal at the tennis in Paris.

Boyfriend and girlfriend Tomas Machac and Katerina Siniakova beat Xinyu Wang and Zhizhen Zhang of China in a deciding tiebreak, 6-2 5-7 [10-8].

There was speculation that the Czech stars broke up ahead of the tournament, but they shared a kiss after winning gold.

What a night so far for the hosts! Swimming, BMX, and maybe now football.

In the quarter-final, they lead Argentina 1-0 with just 15 minutes left.

Crystal Palace striker Jean-Philippe Mateta has the only goal sofar, heading in after only five minutes.

Beth Shriever just had a terrible start in the BMX racing final, and finished last.

Gold goes to Australia's Saya Sakakibara, with Manon Veenstra of the Netherlands taking silver. Switzerland's Zoe Claessens won bronze.

The champion from Tokyo got caught up in early traffic and couldn't recover.

It followed Kye White suffering a horror crash to end his hopes in the men's final.

READ MORE HERE.

Novak Djokovic is now just one game away from a first ever Olympic gold medal.

The world No.2 beat Lorenzo Musetti 6-4 6-2 and will faceCarlos Alcarazon Sunday’s - a rematch of theWimbledonfinal.

READ MORE HERE.

Two silvers in a matter of minutes for Team GB!

Duncan Scott comes second in men's 200m individual medley. Home hero Leon Marchand wins it in a raucous atmosphere, a fourth gold of the Games.

That's EIGHT Olympic medals for Scott. A Team GB legend.

Ben Proud takes the silver medal in the men's 50m freestyle.

Australia's Cam McEvoy took gold and Florent Manaudou of France claimed bronze. A thrilling race.



Keely Hodgkinson wins her 800m heat with room to spare.

She is now safely through to the semi-finals, which take place on Sunday. Another Team GB member, 17-year-old Phoebe Gill, will join her.

Team GB are safely into the next round of the Mixed 4x400m relay with Sam Reardon, Laviai Nielsen, Alex Haydock-Wilson and Nicole Yeargin setting a new British record.

In the previous heat however, the Amerixan quartet set a new world record. Expect another one tomorrow too.

Here comes Novak Djokovic to take on Italy's Lorenzo Musetti.

The winner of course, faces Carlos Alcaraz in the final. A remonder that Novak Djokovic has never won Olympic gold....

So sit tight for the evening folks, here are the main gold medal hopes for Team GB.....

7.30pm:Men's 50m Freestyle Final (Ben Proud);7.36pm:Women's 200m Backstroke Final (Katie Shanahan, Honey Osrin);7.43pm:Men's 200m Individual Medley Final (Duncan Scott),8.35pm:BMX Racing Men's Final (Kye Whyte);8.50pm:BMX Racing Women's Final (Beth Shriver).

Relaxed? No, us neither.

It's gold for Teddy Riner in the men's 100kg category!

The Frenchman sinks to his knees after defeating Kim Min-jong of South Korea and his know the most successful judoka in Olympic history.

He has four gold medals and two bronzes, and the 35-year-old could add another tomorrow in the mixed team event.

There was drama in the women's 100 metres heats after South Sudan athlete Lucia Moris fell before screaming in pain.

But she was attended to by a rival before being stretchered away.

READ MORE HERE.



There's the men's kayak cross time trials done and dusted.

Joe Clarke, who agonisingly missed out on the medals in the kayak singles yesterday, has qualified for the first round of the kayak cross in the quickest time.

Clarke notched 66.08 seconds. Team GB's Adam Burgess goes through in 26th place.



World Sailing have confirmed today's windsurfing men's and women's finals have been postponed until tomorrow.

Britain's Emma Wilson is in gold medal position in the women's table, but her date with destiny has been put on hold.

Team GB have won their third gold medal of the day with a brilliant triumph in the team showjumping event.

Ben Maher, Harry Charles and Scott Brash have emerged victorious in dramatic fashion to edge out the United States at the top of the standings.

It sees Team GB climb to third place in the overall medal table, putting them behind only the United States and leaders China.



Wimbledon champion Carlos Alcaraz is into the Olympic men's singles finalafter thrashing Felix Auger-Aliassime in straight sets.

He needed just 75 minutes to win their semi-final 6-1 6-1 to set up a potential showdown with Novak Djokovic for the gold medal.

If the Spaniard wins the top prize, he will become the youngest men's singles champion in history at the age of just 21.

Team GB gymnast Bryony Page has taken the gold medal with a stunning display in the women's trampoline final.

She went into her routine knowing she had to top Viyaleta Bardzilouskaya's score of 56.060 to take the lead and she did just that.

Her winning score of 56.48 sees Team GB claim their eighth gold medal of the Olympics and their second of the day.

Andy Murray was given a hero's welcome by his Team GB colleagues after playing his final ever match last night.

The two-time Wimbledon champion featured alongside Dan Evans in the men's doubles as they were knocked out by American duo Tommy Paul and Taylor Fritz.

A very warm welcome for a very special person. \u2764\ufe0fAndy Murray received a guard of honour from Team GB as he arrived back. \ud83e\udd1d#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/iBUA5PDRH0

Slovakian swimmer Tamara Potockawas carried away on a stretcher and given oxygenafter taking part in the women's 200-metre individual medley.

Her legs buckled as she climbed out of the pool before tumbling to the ground and being immediately swarmed by medics.

The 21-year-old was later taken away on a stretcher and was spotted with an oxygen mask as she left the arena.

The venue's media manager confirmed that Potocka was conscious and under medical assessment, according to Reuters.

It has since been reported byThe Athleticthat she suffered an asthma attack.

Emily Craig and Imogen Grant have taken gold for Team GB with a dominant display in the women's lightweight double sculls final.

They were somewhat slow to start but took the lead at the 400 metre mark and never looked back as they cruised to victory.

Romania have taken the silver medal with Greece finishing in third place.



Anthony Harding and Jack Laugher have won bronze for Team GB in the men's three-metre synchronised platform diving final.

Daoyi Long and Zongyuan Wang take gold for China with Mexican duo Juan Celaya and Osmar Olvera claiming the silver medal.

It was a very strong display from Harding and Laugher but nobody was going to catch China at the top.

Mexico also did brilliantly well to clinch silver with a series of outstanding dives.

Ollie Wynne-Griffith and Tom George have been forced to settle for silver in the men's pair final after being pipped to Olympic gold at the last second.

They looked set to secure Team GB's seventh gold medal of the Games until a late surge from Croatian brothers Valent and Martin Sinkovic saw them slip to second place.

Moe Sbihi, a former Olympic champion rower who was on commentary duties forBBCSport, said: "They put themselves in that hurt locker for so long in that race.

"They did 99 per cent of that right. It was just heartbreak for them. It's a great advert, again, for the last stroke that always counts."

Adam Peaty will not take part in the mixed 4x100m medley relay heats for Team GB this morning.

The three-time Olympic gold medallist tested positive for Covid-19 a day after winning silver in the 100m breaststroke.

He could still represent Team GB in the finals if they manage to qualify despite missing the heats.

Peaty could also compete in the men’s 4x100m medley relay on Saturday if he is deemed fit enough to take to the pool.



The only transgender boxer at the Olympics has called for fighters who fail gender tests to be banned.

It comes after Imane Khelif, who has XY chromosomes, sparked controversy in her 46-second win over Italian opponent Angela Carini.

Hergie Bacyadan, who identifies as a transgender man,said: "In sparring it's OK but if they have XY chromosomes in competition they should abide by the rules.

"I am trans at heart, but I did not take any hormones or steroids to look like a man. If I compete in the women's division, I don't think there's an issue."

American rapper Flavor Flav has paid Veronica Fraley's rent after the discus thrower revealed she was struggling to make ends meet despite being an Olympic athlete.

She wrote on social media: "I compete in the Olympic Games TOMORROW and can’t even pay my rent.

"My school only sent about 75 per cent of my rent while they pay football players (who haven’t won anything) enough to buy new cars and houses."

Flav, a founding member of Public Enemy, replied: ""I gotchu. DM me and I’ll send payment TODAY so you don’t have to worry about it TOMORROW and imma be rooting for ya tomorrow."

It was later reported byNBCthat Flav had paid Fraley's rent in full.

Here's a look at some of the biggest events taking place on day seven of the Games...

9.42am: Rowing - men's pair finals

9.54am: Rowing - women's pair finals

10.00am: Diving - men's 3km synchronised springboard final

10.06am: Rowing - men's lightweight double sculls finals

10.18am: Rowing - women's lightweight double sculls finals

12.50pm: Trampoline gymnastics - women's final

1.00pm: Equestrian - jumping team final

3.00pm: Judo - women's 78kg final and men's 100kg final

6.00pm: Tennis - mixed doubles gold medal match

6.50pm: Trampoline gymnastics - men's final

7.30pm: Swimming - men's 50m freestyle final

7.36pm: Swimming - women's 200m backstroke final

7.43pm: Swimming - men's 200m individual medley final

8.20pm: Athletics - men's 10km final

8.35pm: Cycling (BMX) - men’s final

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Hungarian boxer Anna Luca Hamori has insisted that she doesn't care about the controversy surrounding her next opponent Imane Khelif.

Khelif won her first bout on Thursday when Angela Carini of Italy quit after just 46 seconds, prompting an outcry on social media.

Hamori, however, is not worried about the prospect of facing the Algerian fighter tomorrow.

"I’m not scared," she said. "I don’t care about the press story and social media. If she or he is a man, it will be a bigger victory for me if I win.

"I’m trying to not use my phone before the fight. I don’t want to care about the comments or the story or the news.

"I just want to stay focused on myself. I did it before my last two fights, so I think this is the key. We will see."



Hello and welcome to Express Sport's rolling coverage of the latest news from the Olympics.

There were no golds for Team GB on Thursday but there will be more opportunities across the action-packed schedule on day seven.

We'll be bringing you the biggest stories from the Games throughout the day, so grab yourself a coffee and stay tuned...

title: Olympics news: Imane Khelif row takes fresh twist as Team GB win three gold medals | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs, Fraser Watson
description: Express Sport is on hand to bring you the latest news on day seven of the Olympics.
